self_contained_note: This artifact is tracked inside the standalone UltrafastSecp256k1 repository (docs/benchmark_artifacts/). All raw aggregate cells, resource values, correctness counts, and metadata needed to read the measurement are embedded directly in this JSON -- it is a self-contained measurement record, not a reproduction script. The capture harness that produced these numbers (benchmarks/secondary_invariant_constants/opencl_generator_w4_ab/ and related run_generator_w4.sh, referenced below in provenance/reproduction fields) lives in the outer multi-repo workspace and is NOT shipped in this standalone library repository -- those paths are external-workspace provenance only, not library-local reproduction commands. It mirrors the fuller working note at workingdocs/secondary_invariant_constants/opencl_generator_w4_production_claude_v4.json, which is itself an outer-workspace file (outside this standalone repository, local-only) and adds a re-verified in-context measurement cell table captured on 2026-07-12.

callers: BIP-352 pipeline kernels (src/opencl/kernels/secp256k1_bip352.cl, via #include of secp256k1_extended.cl)
claim.description: scalar_mul_generator_windowed_impl (window w=4 precomputed generator table {0*G..15*G}) used to declare 'AffinePoint table[16]' as an unqualified function-scope array. In OpenCL C 1.2, an unqualified array declared inside a function body is PRIVATE address-space, per-work-item memory -- it is NOT __local (work-group-shared) memory. The table was rebuilt via 128 individual per-limb literal assignments on every kernel-thread invocation. The table is now declared exactly once at OpenCL program scope in __constant address space as GENERATOR_TABLE_W4[16] and read by index. This is a storage-only optimization: scalar-nibble extraction, doubling-and-add control flow, and point arithmetic (point_double_unchecked / point_from_affine / point_add_mixed_unchecked call sequence) are unchanged; only WHERE the 16-entry table lives changed.

hardware.host_compiler: g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -I/usr/include (benchmarks/secondary_invariant_constants/opencl_generator_w4_ab/host.cpp -- external-workspace-only, not present in this standalone library repository)
hardware.device_compiler: NVIDIA OpenCL device compiler via clBuildProgram

private_memory_bytes.note: Measured directly against the real, shipped production secp256k1_extended.cl kernel (generator_mul_windowed_PRODUCTION build), not a benchmark-only copy. The logical table itself is 16*sizeof(AffinePoint)=16*64=1024 bytes; the reported private-mem footprint is 32 bytes larger than that on both sides, presumably other promoted locals/spill accounting by the NVIDIA OpenCL compiler.
private_memory_bytes.raw_capture_file_external_workspace_prov: external-workspace-only, NOT present in this standalone library repository: benchmarks/secondary_invariant_constants/opencl_generator_w4_ab/resource_result.txt (outer multi-repo workspace, captured during a prior task session)

correctness.oracle: Independent from-scratch Python secp256k1 EC scalar multiplication (affine double-and-add, y^2=x^3+7 mod p, SEC2 domain parameters), imports no project code (benchmarks/secondary_invariant_constants/opencl_generator_w4_ab/oracle_ecmul.py -- external-workspace-only, not present in this standalone library repository).
correctness.method: generator_mul_windowed_op_a / generator_mul_windowed_op_b / generator_mul_windowed_PRODUCTION OpenCL kernels, one scalar_mul_generator_windowed call per thread (matches production generator_mul_windowed shape exactly, no chaining), compared against the independent oracle and against each other.

raw_capture_files_external_workspace_pro.note: external-workspace-only, NOT present in this standalone library repository (outer multi-repo workspace, captured during a prior task session)

in_context_benchmark.method: clGetEventProfilingInfo wall-time, 5 independent runs x 7 profiled passes per (batch) cell, scale-aware iteration count calibrated once per cell from a real single-iteration measurement on this device (target ~20ms/pass), same iteration count used for op A (private array) and op B (constant table) at that cell.
in_context_benchmark.production_claim: batch=1024 approximately -0.43% (noise-level, not a regression); batch=65536 approximately -9.71%; batch=1,048,576 approximately -10.35% in-context wall-time improvement. All three cells have non-overlapping baseline/new ranges and <3% run-to-run variance on both sides.
in_context_benchmark.raw_capture_file_external_workspace_prov: external-workspace-only, NOT present in this standalone library repository: benchmarks/secondary_invariant_constants/opencl_generator_w4_ab/run_log.txt (outer multi-repo workspace, captured during a prior task session)

isolated_microbenchmark_excluded_from_pr.note: Isolated mode measures ONLY the table build/access step plus one point_from_affine call in total complete isolation -- it is NOT amortized against the ~64 point_double_unchecked / point_add_mixed_unchecked calls that dominate real generator_mul_windowed runtime. These deltas are diagnostic only and are NEVER cited as the production throughput claim (per this repository's absolute benchmark-evidence rule).
isolated_microbenchmark_excluded_from_pr.raw_capture_file_external_workspace_prov: external-workspace-only, NOT present in this standalone library repository: benchmarks/secondary_invariant_constants/opencl_generator_w4_ab/run_log_isolated.txt (outer multi-repo workspace, captured during a prior task session)

reproduction.note: The ab_bench capture harness that produced the cells/values above is NOT shipped in this standalone library repository -- it lives only in the outer multi-repo workspace used to develop this optimization. The paths below are external-workspace provenance records (documenting how the numbers were originally captured), not library-local reproduction commands: nothing under these paths exists inside this repository's checkout.
reproduction.library_local_alternative: The static gate ci/check_opencl_generator_w4.py (in this repository) independently recomputes the 16 canonical GENERATOR_TABLE_W4 values and verifies the storage-only source-scan invariants; it does not re-run the GPU timing capture.
